videoProcess.title: Turn one character idea into a sprite sheet preview.
videoProcess.description: Preview how a prompt becomes a 2D character sheet and export metadata.
videoProcess.fallback: Your browser does not support the video tag.

howItWorksProcess.title: Generate a usable sprite sheet in three steps.
howItWorksProcess.description: Describe or upload a character, choose the output shape, then inspect the sheet before using it in your engine.

step-1.title: Describe or upload the character.
step-1.description: Start with one prompt or a reference image. Keep the first request focused on character identity and movement style.

step-2.title: Choose the export platform.
step-2.description: Pick Unity or Godot under one Export Platform selector, then choose the action pack your prototype needs.

step-3.title: Generate and inspect the sheet.
step-3.description: Review the generated sprite sheet, metadata, and task status before cleanup or download.

scrollStack.title: AI Sprite Generator keeps the first asset pass fast.
scrollStack.description: Explore the prompt, sprite sheet, export files, and credit plans.

card-1.title: Prompt-to-sprite generation
card-1.description: Turn one character description into a consistent sheet preview.
subtext: The generator is designed for prototypes, game jams, and pre-production decisions.
Prompts stay focused on readable 2D motion rather than generic AI art.
The generated result can be reviewed before any heavier export work.

card-2.title: Reference image support
card-2.description: Upload a sketch or existing character to guide generation.
subtext: Reference image support helps preserve the character direction.
Upload stays lightweight inside the generator.
The user can retry without rebuilding the whole request.

card-3.title: Action pack presets
card-3.description: Choose platformer, top-down, action RPG, or a custom pack.
subtext: Preset packs keep the request simple and practical.
Custom notes handle unusual poses without crowding the main input.
The generator keeps one clear job on the page.

card-4.title: Export platform selector
card-4.description: Group Unity and Godot under one export decision.
subtext: Unity and Godot are not separate hero buttons.
The platform choice shapes metadata and downstream packaging.
The interface stays compact while still supporting engine intent.

card-5.title: Pricing that matches usage
card-5.description: Use credits for image generation cost control.
subtext: Free users can try the generator before paying.
Pro plans are aimed at active prototype iteration.
Credits keep image generation cost predictable.

card-6.title: Generate on the page
card-6.description: Create a sprite pack directly from the page without opening a separate dashboard.
subtext: Describe the character and choose the export platform in one place.
Review the result before downloading files.
Use the same page when you want another pass.

gallery.title: Sprite sheets and engine previews.
gallery.description: Use concrete generated assets instead of abstract decoration so builders can judge the output quickly.

pricingProcess.title: Credit-based plans for prototype velocity.
pricingProcess.description: Choose Starter or Pro, then switch between monthly and yearly billing.

faqProcess.title: Questions answered before you generate.
faqProcess.description: Clear answers about AI Sprite Generator, credits, exports, and review.

item-1.question: Who is AI Sprite Generator for?
item-1.answer: It is for indie game developers, solo builders, and small teams that need fast 2D sprite sheets for prototypes, game jams, and vertical slices.

item-2.question: Can I upload a reference image?
item-2.answer: Yes. The generator supports a lightweight reference upload so the generated sprite sheet can follow an existing character direction.

item-3.question: Does it support Unity and Godot?
item-3.answer: Yes. Unity and Godot are grouped under Export Platform because they are one output decision, not separate primary actions.

item-4.question: Is this final production art?
item-4.answer: AI Sprite Generator is designed for prototypes and pre-production. Final production assets should still be reviewed and polished by a human artist.

item-5.question: How does pricing work?
item-5.answer: AI Sprite Generator uses a credit-based model because image generation has real per-use cost. Starter is $12/month, Pro is $29/month, and yearly billing uses the 10-month price.

item-6.question: Can I download engine packs?
item-6.answer: The generator focuses on sheet preview and downloadable files, including the export details needed for inspection.

item-7.question: Can I use the output commercially?
item-7.answer: Paid plans are positioned for commercial prototype use, but generated assets should still be checked for originality and legal suitability.

item-8.question: Is Google login the only login method?
item-8.answer: Yes. AI Sprite Generator keeps authentication simple and only exposes Google login.

item-9.question: Can I use it from this page?
item-9.answer: Yes. The main page is built for generating, reviewing, and downloading sprite packs without sending you through extra screens.

ctaProcess.title: Generate your first sprite pack today.
ctaProcess.description: Describe one character, pick the export platform, and inspect the generated sheet before downloading the files.
